frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: You know, sometimes I really think about how much my mother did for us. Every morning, fresh, hot parathas and sabzi, even after a long day of chores. Never saw her complain, always cooking with so much love. Woh 'maa ke haath ka khana' ka taste... it's just something else, yaar. Aajkal toh kids mostly eat outside or instant food. They don't know what real ghar ka khana is, made with that kind of selfless dedication. 🙏
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment glorifies the mother's domestic labor ('cooking with so much love', 'selfless dedication') without acknowledging it as work, framing it instead as a pure act of love and sacrifice. It implicitly compares 'maa ke haath ka khana' with modern 'instant food', thereby setting an unattainable, gendered standard for domestic care for contemporary women/wives and subtly suggesting their deficiency.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
